acts.name: Whispers on the Waterfront
beats: The player arrives at their starting location and learns that a British informant has compromised tonight's plan — the exact hour of action was leaked.
Initial interviews with NPCs reveal surface-level alibis and competing suspicions; Paul Revere points a finger at the nervous newcomer Elias Croft.
The player discovers the first environmental clue — a torn page or overheard conversation — that begins to complicate the obvious suspect.

acts.name: Ink and Coin
beats: Deeper investigation at the Green Dragon Tavern and the counting house reveals financial irregularities tied to one of the inner circle.
The coded letter is discovered, written in a cipher used only by those with access to Crown correspondence — narrowing the suspect pool dramatically.
Dr. Joseph Warren grows agitated when questioned about his recent meetings, providing misdirection; meanwhile the payment receipt surfaces at an unexpected location.
The pressure events escalate — British activity on the harbor becomes visible, and the player feels the clock tightening.

acts.name: The Harbor Reckons
beats: All evidence threads converge — the player must confront the true culprit, Thomas Dillworth, with the coded letter and payment receipt.
Dillworth attempts to flee toward the Custom House or deflect blame onto Dr. Warren; the player must act decisively.
Final accusation scene: Samuel Adams presides as the player presents their case — success sends the tea party forward, failure brings the redcoats down on Griffin's Wharf.

goal: Identify and expose the spy within the Sons of Liberty before the British intercept the tea party operation at Griffin's Wharf.
name: The Traitor at Griffin's Wharf
premise: On the night the Sons of Liberty plan their most daring act of defiance, a mole inside their inner circle has sold their secrets to the Royal Governor. The player must move through the fog-wrapped streets and crowded meeting halls of colonial Boston to expose the traitor before British forces close the trap.
openingSituation: Samuel Adams has just received word that Customs Officer Hartwell knew the meeting time before it was publicly announced — someone in the room talked. The Sons of Liberty have one hour before the ships must be boarded.
